description.en: The dough behind Naples' UNESCO-listed pizza: just flour, water, a little yeast and salt, fermented up to 24 hours before being stretched by hand and flash-baked 60-90 seconds in a wood oven near 450°C. The long ferment matters more than the toppings.
description.de: Der Teig hinter Neapels UNESCO-gelisteter Pizza: nur Mehl, Wasser, ein Hauch Hefe und Salz, bis zu 24 Stunden fermentiert, von Hand gezogen und in 60-90 Sekunden im Holzofen bei rund 450°C gebacken. Nicht der Belag, sondern die lange Gare ist die eigentliche Kunst.
description.es: La masa de la pizza napolitana, patrimonio de la UNESCO: solo harina, agua, un toque de levadura y sal, fermentada hasta 24 horas, estirada a mano y horneada en 60-90 segundos en horno de leña a unos 450°C. El oficio está en la larga fermentación, no en los ingredientes.
description.fr: La pâte de la pizza napolitaine, classée à l'UNESCO: juste farine, eau, une pointe de levure et du sel, fermentée jusqu'à 24 heures, étirée à la main et cuite en 60-90 secondes dans un four à bois à environ 450°C. Le vrai savoir-faire tient à la fermentation, pas à la garniture.

origin.note: Governed by the AVPN's international disciplinare; the art of Neapolitan pizza-making (Pizzaiuolo) is inscribed on UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list.

ingredients.note: kept minimal for a long 8-24 hour ferment

yield.shape: hand-stretched disc with a thick, puffy cornicione (rim)

keeping.note: This entry is for the dough itself, eaten minutes after baking; it doesn't keep like a loaf, but unbaked dough balls freeze well for later.

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Dissolve a little yeast in the water, then work in the flour by hand, keeping the salt from touching the yeast directly for more than a few minutes.

method.title: Knead
method.summary: Work the dough by hand into a smooth, elastic mass, about 20 minutes.

method.title: Bulk rest
method.summary: Let the whole mass rest a couple of hours at room temperature before dividing.

method.title: Ball and ferment long
method.summary: Divide into 200-280 g balls and let them ferment together at room temperature for 8 to 24 hours until soft, domed and airy.

method.title: Stretch by hand
method.summary: Press and stretch each ball outward from the center with fingertips only, never a rolling pin, leaving a thicker rim to puff up in the oven.

method.title: Flash-bake
method.summary: Launch onto a screaming-hot stone or wood-fired oven floor at 430-480°C and bake just 60-90 seconds until the rim blisters and chars in spots.

source.adaptation: Grams scaled down from the disciplinare's official per-liter-of-water ratios to a home batch of four 250 g dough balls.
